title: Decimal multiplication place value
description: Understand multiplying decimals as multiplying whole numbers and then adjusting for place value.
topic_path: Module 2: Arithmetic operations including dividing by a fraction
Topic B: Multi‐digit decimal operations—adding, subtracting, and multiplying

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $0.034 \times 4.8$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.034} \times \maroonD{4.8} &= \blueE{\dfrac{34}{1{,}000}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{48}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{34 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{48 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.
The expression $34\times 48 \times \dfrac{1}{10{,}000}$ is equivalent to $0.034 \times 4.8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $2.3 \times 5.01$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{2.3} \times \maroonD{5.01} &= \blueE{\dfrac{23}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{501}{100}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{23 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{501 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.
The expression $23\times 501 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}$ is equivalent to $2.3 \times 5.01$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $5.9 \times 8.7$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{5.9} \times \maroonD{8.7} &= \blueE{\dfrac{59}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{87}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{59 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{87 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.
The expression $59\times 87 \times \dfrac{1}{100}$ is equivalent to $5.9 \times 8.7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $4.11 \times 0.055$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{4.11} \times \maroonD{0.055} &= \blueE{\dfrac{411}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{55}{1{,}000}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{411 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{55 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.
The expression $411\times 55 \times \dfrac{1}{100{,}000}$ is equivalent to $4.11 \times 0.055$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $0.12 \times 0.7$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.12} \times \maroonD{0.7} &= \blueE{\dfrac{12}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{12 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{7 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.12} \times \maroonD{0.7} &= \blueE{\dfrac{12}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{12 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{7 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{12}  \times \maroonD{7} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&={12}  \times {7} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}} 
\end{align}$

The numbers $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}$ and $\goldE{1{,}000}$ are reciprocals of each other. So *multiplying* by $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}$ is the same as *dividing* by $\goldE{1{,}000}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.12} \times \maroonD{0.7} &= \blueE{\dfrac{12}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{7}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{12 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{7 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{12}  \times \maroonD{7} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&={12}  \times {7} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}  \\\\
&= 12\times 7 \div \goldE{1{,}000}
\end{align}$
The expression $12\times 7 \div \goldE{1{,}000}$ is equivalent to $0.12 \times 0.7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $106 \times 3.7$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{106} \times \maroonD{3.7} &= \blueE{106} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{37}{10}}\\\\
&=\blueE{106} \times \left(\maroonD{37 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.
The expression $106\times 37 \div \goldE{10}$ is equivalent to $106 \times 3.7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $0.8 \times 12.4$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.8} \times \maroonD{12.4} &= \blueE{\dfrac{8}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{124}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{8 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{124 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.8} \times \maroonD{12.4} &= \blueE{\dfrac{8}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{124}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{8 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{124 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{8}  \times \maroonD{124} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&={8}  \times {124} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100}} 
\end{align}$

The numbers $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100}}$ and $\goldE{100}$ are reciprocals of each other. So *multiplying* by $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100}}$ is the same as *dividing* by $\goldE{100}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{0.8} \times \maroonD{12.4} &= \blueE{\dfrac{8}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{124}{10}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{8 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{124 \times \dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{8}  \times \maroonD{124} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{10}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{10}}\right)\\\\
&={8}  \times {124} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100}} \\\\
&= 8\times 124 \div \goldE{100}
\end{align}$
The expression $8\times 124 \div \goldE{100}$ is equivalent to $0.8 \times 12.4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following expressions is equivalent to $4.56 \times 0.031$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's rewrite the decimals in fraction form. Then we can use the properties of multiplication to write an equivalent expression.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{4.56} \times \maroonD{0.031} &= \blueE{\dfrac{456}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{31}{1{,}000}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{456 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{31 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)
\end{align}$

We are multiplying, so we can use the commutative and associative properties. Let's rearrange and group the fractions.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{4.56} \times \maroonD{0.031} &= \blueE{\dfrac{456}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{31}{1{,}000}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{456 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{31 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{456}  \times \maroonD{31} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)\\\\
&={456}  \times {31} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100{,}000}}
\end{align}$

The numbers $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100{,}000}}$ and $\goldE{100{,}000}$ are reciprocals of each other. So *multiplying* by $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100{,}000}}$ is the same as *dividing* by $\goldE{100{,}000}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{4.56} \times \maroonD{0.031} &= \blueE{\dfrac{456}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{31}{1{,}000}}\\\\
&=\left(\blueE{456 \times \dfrac{1}{100}}\right) \times \left(\maroonD{31 \times \dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)\\\\
&=\blueE{456}  \times \maroonD{31} \times \left(\blueE{\dfrac{1}{100}} \times \maroonD{\dfrac{1}{1{,}000}}\right)\\\\
&={456}  \times {31} \times \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{100{,}000}} \\\\
&= 456\times 31 \div \goldE{100{,}000}
\end{align}$
The expression $456\times 31 \div \goldE{100{,}000}$ is equivalent to $4.56 \times 0.031$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The product $796 \times 68$ equals $54{,}128$.

**Use the previous fact to evaluate as a decimal.**

$7.96 \times 6.8 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Instead of $796\times 68$, the second product is $796\,\text{hundredths} \times 68\,\text{tenths}$.

We can group the fraction factors to see how the second product relates to the first product.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
